---
title: "Day 4 — Exercise: Per-contig stats with `noodles-fasta`"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-copy: true
    code-overflow: wrap
    highlight-style: github
---

## Goal

Add the [`noodles-fasta`](https://docs.rs/noodles-fasta/) crate as a dependency, then write a function that streams records from a [`BufRead`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/io/trait.BufRead.html) source and produces per-contig statistics:

```rust
struct ContigStat { name: String, length: usize, gc: f64 }
fn fasta_stats<R: std::io::BufRead>(reader: R) -> std::io::Result<Vec<ContigStat>>
```

This is your first exercise with a **real external crate** doing the heavy lifting. Compare the body of your function with the hand-rolled FASTA stretch goal from [day 1](../day1/01-gc-content.qmd).

## The crate

Open `Cargo.toml` and confirm the dependency block reads:

```toml
[dependencies]
noodles-fasta = "0.50"
```

Then in code:

```rust
use noodles_fasta as fasta;
```

The [crate docs](https://docs.rs/noodles-fasta/) are your reference. You will need:

- [`fasta::io::Reader::new`](https://docs.rs/noodles-fasta/latest/noodles_fasta/io/struct.Reader.html#method.new) to wrap any `BufRead`
- [`reader.records()`](https://docs.rs/noodles-fasta/latest/noodles_fasta/io/struct.Reader.html#method.records) to get an iterator over `Result<Record>`
- [`record.name()`](https://docs.rs/noodles-fasta/latest/noodles_fasta/record/struct.Record.html#method.name) for the record's name as bytes
- [`record.sequence().as_ref()`](https://docs.rs/noodles-fasta/latest/noodles_fasta/record/struct.Record.html#method.sequence) for the raw sequence as `&[u8]`

## The exercise

```bash
cd day4/ex-fasta-stats
```

Implement `fasta_stats`. Make all four tests pass — they use [`std::io::Cursor`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/io/struct.Cursor.html) to feed in-memory byte strings to the function without touching the filesystem.

## How to run

```bash
cargo test
cargo run -- data/sample.fa
```

## Hints

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="Hint 1 — wrap the reader"}
`noodles-fasta` expects a `BufRead`. Any `BufRead` will do — a `BufReader<File>`, a `Cursor<&[u8]>`, anything implementing the trait. Construct the FASTA reader once at the top of the function:

```rust
let mut reader = fasta::io::Reader::new(reader);
```
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="Hint 2 — iterate records and propagate errors"}
[`records()`](https://docs.rs/noodles-fasta/latest/noodles_fasta/io/struct.Reader.html#method.records) returns an iterator yielding `std::io::Result<Record>`. Use a `for` loop and the [`?` operator](https://doc.rust-lang.org/book/ch09-02-recoverable-errors-with-result.html#a-shortcut-for-propagating-errors-the--operator) to short-circuit on the first error:

```rust
let mut out = Vec::new();
for result in reader.records() {
    let record = result?;
    // ... process record ...
    out.push(stat);
}
Ok(out)
```
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="Hint 3 — name and sequence"}
The record's name (the first whitespace-delimited token after `>`) and sequence are accessed via methods:

```rust
let name = std::str::from_utf8(record.name())
    .map_err(|_| std::io::Error::other("non-UTF-8 record name"))?
    .to_string();
let seq: &[u8] = record.sequence().as_ref();
let length = seq.len();
let gc_count = seq.iter().filter(|&&b| matches!(b, b'G' | b'C' | b'g' | b'c')).count();
let gc = if length == 0 { 0.0 } else { gc_count as f64 / length as f64 };
```

Note `matches!`: a [macro](https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/macro.matches.html) that is shorthand for `match x { pat => true, _ => false }`. Very handy in iterator filters.
:::

## Solution

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Solution"}
See `day4/ex-fasta-stats-solution/src/main.rs` for the full reference. The function is around 15 lines — almost all of it is the GC arithmetic. `noodles-fasta` does every byte of parsing for you.
:::

## Where this leads

- Replace `BufReader<File>` with a `BufReader<flate2::read::GzDecoder<File>>` and your tool works on `.fa.gz` with no other changes. `noodles-fasta` does **not** care: it just wants a `BufRead`.
- **Exercise 03**: same pattern, for FASTQ — but now you also write filtered records back out.
